title: pkcs11 algorithm(): first sign() call blocks tokio executor on HSM I/O
description: secretx-pkcs11/src/lib.rs sign(): calls self.algorithm() which is a sync fn. On the first call (cache cold), detect_algorithm() opens an HSM session via ro_session() — a blocking system call. This runs directly on the tokio executor thread. After the first call, OnceLock caches the result and subsequent calls are instant. Suggested rewrite: call detect_algorithm() eagerly at construction time in from_uri() (after the slot lookup succeeds, the HSM is clearly accessible). This eliminates the blocking-in-async issue entirely and makes algorithm() always instant without the OnceLock.
